---
title: "Journal Entry 7: Overall Project Reflection"
date: "2026-04-30"
categories: [reflection, conclusion]
description: "Overall reflection on the research project and next steps"
---

![Project reflection](../thumbnail.jpg)

## Overall Project Reflection

This project taught me both technical skills and research process
habits. On the technical side I implemented a LLM-driven mutation
pipeline, integrated AST-aware operators, automated test validation with
`pytest`, and used mutation analysis tools like `mutmut`.

Research skills: I designed reproducible experiments, selected metrics
(detection rate, repair success, iterations-to-repair, time-to-repair),
and learned how to communicate threats to validity and ethical
constraints.

Major challenges and how I addressed them:

- Scope vs. rigor: balancing a runnable prototype with rigorous
	evaluation required trade-offs; I focused on a compact workload to
	iterate quickly, while documenting limitations and future scaling
	plans.
- Validation limits: relying on unit tests surfaced overfitting risks;
	I now plan to add metamorphic and specification-based checks.


Proudest accomplishments:

- Delivering a complete, replayable harness (OPENCODE + driver) that others can reuse.
- Producing an experimental write-up that ties implementation details to
	interpretable metrics and concrete recommendations.

Next steps:

- Scale experiments to larger projects and report aggregated statistics
	with confidence intervals.
- Implement stronger validation checks and build a failure-mode
	taxonomy to guide agent improvements.
- Package the harness and documentation so other researchers can replay
	runs and contribute operator implementations.

These entries summarize the learning journey: concrete engineering
progress, iterative scientific thinking, and a clear path forward for
extending the research.
